discussion_comments.comment_body: ah, most of these are defined in custom.d.ts (and for backend types in gotypes.d.ts) so they are already global and auto-imported.
should I make these platform specific? I was wondering if I should make it the windows key on linux, idk
ya, that's a good idea to make them platform specific.

How the system currently works is Cmd is automatically MetaKey on Mac and AltKey on linux and option is the opposite. But if a Keybind specifies meta it's always looking for the meta key. kind of like how you can use key code to specify a physical key, I currently have it so meta and alt allow someone to override any platform specific behavior. Does that make sense? It's definitely a niche use case but I figured there's no reason not to have it

Also, in this case the other key would be ignored, so like if for some reason there is a keybinding that is `Meta:Cmd:c` that is interpreted as `Meta:c`, and `Alt:Option:c` is interpreted as `Option:c`. I feel like that is the most reasonable way to interpret those cases anyways
How does this play on Linux? I think we should add an OS check here
Also will this reset on start every time?
oops, i had two commits not pushed to github.  that Cmd+F4 was just testing (removed in the final).  I also have a check for linux which switches between Cmd (for MacOS), and Alt (for Linux/Windows).
